content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi, OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
127	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
128	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
129	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
130	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
131	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
132	
133	### Subagents
134	
135	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	
139	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
140	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
141	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
142	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
143	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
144	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
145	
146	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
147	
148	These run automatically between phases:
149	
150	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
151	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
152	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

10	/-!
11	# The rational-curve isomorphism `C ≅ ℙ¹` (RR.4)
12	
13	This file is the **RR.4** sub-build chapter for the project's headline
14	`genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (the "smooth proper geometrically irreducible
15	genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is isomorphic to `ℙ¹`" lemma pinned in
16	`AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity:290`).
17	
18	The Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 chain for the genus-`0` ⇒ `ℙ¹` classification routes
19	through the four sub-build chapters:
20	
21	- `RR.1` (`WeilDivisor.lean`): the Weil divisor group `Div(C)`, the degree
22	  map `deg : Div(C) → ℤ`, and the degree-zero principal-divisor identity.
23	- `RR.2` (`RRFormula.lean`): the Euler-characteristic identity
24	  `χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g` and Riemann–Roch in genus zero.
25	- `RR.3` (`OCofP.lean`): the line bundle `𝒪_C([P])` of a closed point and
26	  the existence of a non-constant rational function
27	  `f ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C([P])) ∖ k̄ · 1`.
28	- **`RR.4` (this file)**: the global classification — the linear-system
29	  morphism `C ⟶ ℙ¹` produced from `(1, f)` (Pin 1), its degree identified
30	  via the pole divisor (Pin 2), and the degree-`1` ⇒ isomorphism step
31	  (Pin 3). The headline target `AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero_curve_iso_P1`
32	  (Pin 4) is the existing pinned declaration at
33	  `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean:290` — see §0 below.
34	
35	## Status (iter-177 file-skeleton; iter-181 Pin 3 signature refinement)
36	
37	This file was originally the **iter-177 Lane 8** file-skeleton. Each
38	of the three new pinned declarations carries the *intended*
39	substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
40	`blueprint/src/chapters/RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso.tex`) with a
41	`sorry` body. Bodies are iter-182+ work after the RR.1/RR.2/RR.3
42	chain matures (in particular `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal_degree_zero`
43	and the `H⁰(C, 𝒪_C([P]))`-non-constant existence corollary of `RR.3`).
44	
45	iter-181 Lane I refined the Pin 3 signature of
46	`iso_of_degree_one`: the abstract function-field-iso existence
47	hypothesis is replaced by the `[Algebra K(C') K(C)]` typeclass binder
48	+ `Module.finrank K(C') K(C) = 1` equation, matching what the
49	iter-182+ body needs (per `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md`
50	Decision 2 DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY).
51	
52	The 3 new pinned declarations are:
53	
54	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphismToP1OfGlobalSections` — from a
55	   graded `k̄`-algebra hom `k̄[X₀, X₁] →+* Γ(X, ⊤)` whose image of the
56	   irrelevant ideal generates the unit ideal, produce the morphism
57	   `X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` via `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` lifted to
58	   the slice category over `Spec k̄`.
59	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor` — for a
60	   non-constant morphism `φ : C ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` from a smooth
61	   proper curve, extract a positive-degree Weil divisor on `C` (the
62	   pole divisor `φ^*[∞]`).
63	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.iso_of_degree_one` — a non-constant
64	   morphism between smooth proper geometrically irreducible curves
65	   whose induced function-field map is a `k̄`-algebra iso is an
66	   isomorphism of schemes (Hartshorne I.6.12 specialised to
67	   degree-`1`).
68	
69	The headline pin (Pin 4) is satisfied by the existing AVR.lean target.
70	
71	## Note on type expressivity
72	
73	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
74	each pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
75	
76	* `morphismToP1OfGlobalSections` returns a *concrete* morphism
77	  `X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` of `Over (Spec k̄)`, not `Iso.refl _` or a
78	  reflexive placeholder; the input data is the exact pair
79	  `(f, hf)` consumed by Mathlib's `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`.
80	* `morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor` asserts the existence of a
81	  positive-integer degree `d` together with a Weil divisor `D` on `C`
82	  realising it — the *named substantive content* of the degree-via-pole
83	  identification. Unfolds to `∃ d D, 0 < d ∧ D.degree = d`, not `True`.
84	* `iso_of_degree_one` asserts `Nonempty (C ≅ C')` from the
85	  non-constant hypothesis together with the *substantive* degree-`1`
86	  hypothesis `Module.finrank C'.functionField C.functionField = 1`
87	  (paired with an `[Algebra C'.functionField C.functionField]` typeclass
88	  binder for the canonical `φ`-induced function-field map). The
89	  `Nonempty`-wrapped iso is the exact downstream-consumer shape,
90	  matching the `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` headline signature in AVR.lean.
91	  This signature was refined in iter-181 Lane I from the iter-177
92	  file-skeleton placeholder `Nonempty (C'.ff ≃+* C.ff)` to make the
93	  degree-`1`-induced-by-`φ` hypothesis the substantive content; see
94	  `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` Decision 2.
95	
96	Unfolding any pinned declaration exposes the named substantive content
97	(the concrete `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`-derived morphism, the
98	existence-of-positive-degree-divisor statement, the existence of a
99	scheme isomorphism); no `Iso.refl _` / empty-content `proof_wanted` /
100	`Classical.choice ⟨witness⟩` placeholders are used.
101	
102	## Curve conventions
103	
104	Following the project (cf. `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`,
105	`Jacobian.lean`, `OCofP.lean`), a **smooth proper geometrically
106	irreducible curve over `k̄`** carries:
107	
108	- `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]`,
109	- `[IsProper C.hom]`,
110	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]`.
111	
112	These instances are supplied by `inferInstance` at the call site. The
113	`[IsIntegral C.left]` / `[IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]` instances
114	required by the Weil-divisor / `RationalMap.order` API are threaded
115	explicitly per-lemma where consumed.
116

118

122	IV §2 opening paragraph, p. 299 (degree of a finite morphism of
123	curves); I §6 Corollary 6.12, p. 45 (function-field ⇔ smooth projective
124	curve equivalence of categories); II §7 Theorem 7.1 (morphisms to
125	`ℙⁿ` via global sections). Stacks Project tag `0AVX` (degree-`1`
126	finite morphism between integral normal schemes is an isomorphism).

165	/-! ## §1. Pin 1 — the morphism `X ⟶ ℙ¹` from a globally-generating pair
166	(`morphismToP1OfGlobalSections`)
167	
168	Hartshorne II Theorem 7.1, specialised to `ℙ¹` over `k̄`. Given a graded
169	`k̄`-algebra homomorphism `f : k̄[X₀, X₁] →+* Γ(X, ⊤)` whose image of the
170	irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁)` generates the unit ideal of `Γ(X, ⊤)`, the
171	construction `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of Mathlib's
172	`AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic` produces a morphism
173	`X.left ⟶ Proj 𝒜 = ProjectiveLineBarScheme k̄`. Lifting to the slice
174	category over `Spec k̄` (via `Over.homMk` with a section-condition
175	proof) yields the pinned morphism
176	`X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄ : Over (Spec (.of kbar))`. -/
177	
178	/-- **The morphism `X ⟶ ℙ¹_{k̄}` produced from a globally-generating pair of
179	homogeneous sections** (Hartshorne II Theorem 7.1; Mathlib's
180	`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` specialised to the standard
181	ℕ-grading on `k̄[X₀, X₁]`).
182	
183	Given a graded `k̄`-algebra homomorphism `f : k̄[X₀, X₁] →+* Γ(X, ⊤)`, a
184	`k̄`-algebra compatibility witness `halg` (recording that the composite
185	`kbar → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar → Γ(X.left, ⊤)` agrees with the natural
186	algebra map `kbar → Γ(X.left, ⊤)` induced by `X.hom`), and a proof `hf`
187	that the image of the irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁) ⊆ k̄[X₀, X₁]` generates
188	the unit ideal of `Γ(X, ⊤)`, this constructs the unique morphism
189	`φ : X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` characterised on basic-opens by
190	`φ ⁻¹ᵁ D₊(X_i) = X.basicOpen (f X_i)` (the "no-common-zero" expression of
191	Hartshorne's morphism condition). The composite
192	`φ.left ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ = X.hom`, witnessing the `Over (Spec k̄)`
193	section condition.
194	
195	The input data `(f, halg, hf)` is the standard Hartshorne II §7 packaging
196	of "a pair of global sections `s₀, s₁ ∈ H⁰(X, ℒ)` of an invertible sheaf
197	`ℒ` without common zeros": `f` is the graded `k̄`-algebra map sending
198	`X_i ↦ s_i ∈ Γ(X, ℒ) ⊂ Γ(X, ⊤)` (after a choice of trivialisation
199	identifying `H⁰(X, ℒ)` with a submodule of `Γ(X, ⊤)` over the affine
200	cover `X = D(s₀) ∪ D(s₁)`); `halg` is the `kbar`-algebra-compatibility
201	condition (without which the section condition over `Spec kbar` cannot
202	be derived); `hf` is the no-common-zero condition.
203	
204	The construction invokes `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` on the standard
205	grading `projectiveLineBarGrading k̄` (the `ℕ`-grading on
206	`MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄` by total degree, with `GradedRing` instance
207	via `MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra`), then wraps in `Over.homMk` with the
208	section condition proof. The section condition chases through
209	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero` plus
210	`IsScalarTower kbar (𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)` to reduce to
211	`X.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (f.comp MvPolynomial.C)) = X.hom`,
212	which the `halg` hypothesis closes via `Scheme.toSpecΓ_naturality` and
213	`toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv` (cf. the analogous chain in
214	`Genus0BaseObjects/Points.lean:pointOfVec`).
215	
216	Blueprint reference: `lem:morphism_to_p1_from_global_sections`
217	(Hartshorne II Theorem 7.1, II §7). -/
218	noncomputable def morphismToP1OfGlobalSections
219	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar]
220	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
221	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* Γ(X.left, ⊤))
222	    (_halg :
223	      f.comp (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)) =
224	        X.hom.appTop.hom.comp (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv.hom)
225	    (_hf :
226	      Ideal.map f
227	          (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal = ⊤) :
228	    X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
229	  -- Underlying scheme morphism: invoke Mathlib's
230	  -- `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` on `projectiveLineBarGrading kbar`.
231	  -- The result `X.left ⟶ Proj 𝒜 = ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar` is
232	  -- then wrapped via `Over.homMk` with the section condition
233	  -- `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections … ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = X.hom`.
234	  Over.homMk
235	    (Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f _hf) <| by
236	    -- Section condition (`Over (Spec k̄)`-compatibility):
237	    --   `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections … ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = X.hom`
238	    -- where `ProjectiveLineBar.hom = Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map (algebraMap kbar ↥(𝒜 0))`.
239	    haveI : IsScalarTower kbar ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
240	        (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
241	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq
242	        (R := kbar) (S := ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0))
243	        (A := MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) (fun _ => rfl)
244	    change Proj.fromOfGlobalSections _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
245	    rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero, Category.assoc,
246	      ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
247	      ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq kbar, MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
248	    -- Goal: `X.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (f.comp MvPolynomial.C)) = X.hom`.
249	    -- Step 1: convert the `_halg` RingHom equation to a CommRingCat-level equation.
250	    have hcc : CommRingCat.ofHom (f.comp MvPolynomial.C) =
251	        (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ X.hom.appTop := by
252	      rw [← MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq, _halg, CommRingCat.ofHom_comp,
253	        CommRingCat.ofHom_hom, CommRingCat.ofHom_hom]
254	    -- Step 2: rewrite, distribute `Spec.map`, and apply naturality of `toSpecΓ` plus
255	    -- the `toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (ΓSpecIso).inv = 𝟙` identity on `Spec (.of kbar)`.
256	    rw [hcc, Spec.map_comp, ← Category.assoc, ← Scheme.toSpecΓ_naturality,
257	      Category.assoc, toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv, Category.comp_id]
258	
259	/-! ## §2. Pin 2 — the degree of a non-constant `C ⟶ ℙ¹` via its pole divisor
260	(`morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor`)
261	
262	Hartshorne II §6 + IV §2 opening: for a non-constant morphism
263	`φ : C ⟶ ℙ¹` from a smooth proper curve, the function-field extension
264	`k̄(ℙ¹) ↪ K(C)` is finite, and the degree
265	`[K(C) : k̄(ℙ¹)] = deg(φ)` is recovered as the degree of the pullback
266	of any closed point of `ℙ¹` to `C`. Specialising to `Q = ∞`, this
267	identifies `deg(φ)` with the degree of the **pole divisor** of the
268	pulled-back affine coordinate `φ^* u`.
269	
270	For the iter-177 file-skeleton the substantive content is the
271	existence of a positive-degree Weil divisor on `C` realising
272	`deg(φ)`; the body in iter-178+ produces this divisor as the pole
273	divisor of `φ^* u` via the Hartshorne II.6.9 multiplicativity-of-degree
274	identity.
275	
276	This is a *separable* sub-build pin (the lemma is stated independently
277	of the headline `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` and does not require `g(C) = 0`).
278	-/
279	
280	/-- **A local parameter at the closed point `∞ ∈ ℙ¹_{k̄}`** — a non-zero
281	rational function on `(ProjectiveLineBar kbar)` whose order at `∞` is `1`
282	(in Hartshorne's standard affine convention: `t_∞ = X₀ / X₁`, with `X₁` the
283	coordinate trivialising the chart at `∞`). Returned as a subtype packaging
284	the element together with its non-zero witness, for use in
285	`Hom.poleDivisor` below.
286	
287	**iter-187 substrate gap — single named typed sorry.** The substantive
288	choice (the chart-1 ratio coordinate `X₀ / X₁ ∈
289	HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) (X 1)`,
290	embedded into `(ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField`) is iter-188+
291	work. The substrate hooks (per `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin2.md`):
292	- `AlgebraicGeometry.projectiveLineBarAffineCover` chart-`1` open
293	  (`Genus0BaseObjects/BareScheme.lean`).
294	- `HomogeneousLocalization.Away` to extract the global ratio function on
295	  the chart, then `germToFunctionField` to embed in `K(ℙ¹)`.
296	- The non-zero witness is automatic from injectivity of the ratio-as-a-
297	  HomogeneousLocalization-Away-element (the ratio `X₀ / X₁` is not the
298	  zero element of the localised graded ring).
299	
300	This helper is the **single substrate gap** carrying the iter-188 unblock
301	for the consumer scaffold `Hom.poleDivisor_degree_eq_finrank` Steps 1-5
302	below; the gap is **named, typed, and topologically localised**, not a
303	bare body sorry. -/
304	private noncomputable def localParameterAtInfty (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
305	    [IsIntegral (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left] :
306	    { t : (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField // t ≠ 0 } := by
307	  classical
308	  -- The grading on `k̄[X₀, X₁]`.
309	  let 𝒜 : ℕ → Submodule kbar (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
310	    projectiveLineBarGrading kbar
311	  -- The element `f = X 1` is homogeneous of degree 1.
312	  let f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar := MvPolynomial.X 1
313	  have hf : f ∈ 𝒜 1 := MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 1
314	  have hf0 : (MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈ 𝒜 (1 • 1) := by
315	    simpa using MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 0
316	  -- The ratio coordinate `X 0 / X 1 ∈ Away 𝒜 (X 1)` (degree 0).
317	  let x01 : HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f :=
318	    HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk 𝒜 hf 1 (MvPolynomial.X 0) hf0
319	  -- Basic facts about `X 0` and `X 1`.
320	  have hf_ne : f ≠ 0 := MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero 1
321	  have hX0_ne : (MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ≠ 0 :=
322	    MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero 0
323	  -- Step (a): `x01 ≠ 0` in `Away 𝒜 (X 1)`.
324	  have hx01_ne : x01 ≠ 0 := by
325	    intro heq
326	    have hval :
327	        x01.val =
328	          (0 : HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f).val :=
329	      congrArg HomogeneousLocalization.val heq
330	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.val_zero,
331	        HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk] at hval
332	    rw [Localization.mk_eq_mk'_apply,
333	        IsLocalization.mk'_eq_zero_iff] at hval
334	    obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := hval
335	    rcases c.2 with ⟨n, hcn⟩
336	    have hcv_ne : (c : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ≠ 0 := by
337	      simp only [← hcn]
338	      exact pow_ne_zero _ hf_ne
339	    exact hX0_ne ((mul_eq_zero.mp hc).resolve_left hcv_ne)
340	  -- Step (b): `Away 𝒜 (X 1)` is nontrivial (needed for `Nonempty (basicOpen f)`).
341	  haveI hL : Nontrivial (Localization.Away f) :=
342	    (IsLocalization.injective (Localization.Away f)
343	      (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors hf_ne)).nontrivial
344	  haveI hAway : Nontrivial (HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f) := by
345	    refine ⟨1, 0, ?_⟩
346	    intro heq
347	    have hval :
348	        (1 : HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f).val =
349	          (0 : HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f).val :=
350	      congrArg HomogeneousLocalization.val heq
351	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.val_one,
352	        HomogeneousLocalization.val_zero] at hval
353	    exact one_ne_zero hval
354	  -- Step (c): the chart-1 affine open `U = D₊(X 1)` is nonempty (via `awayι`).
355	  let U : (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.Opens := Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f
356	  haveI hU_nonempty : Nonempty U := by
357	    obtain ⟨p⟩ := (inferInstance :
358	        Nonempty (Spec (CommRingCat.of (HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f))))
359	    refine ⟨⟨(Proj.awayι 𝒜 f hf Nat.one_pos).base p, ?_⟩⟩
360	    -- The point lies in the image of `awayι`, which equals `basicOpen 𝒜 f`.
361	    have hrange : (Proj.awayι 𝒜 f hf Nat.one_pos).opensRange = U :=
362	      Proj.opensRange_awayι 𝒜 f hf Nat.one_pos
363	    rw [show U = (Proj.awayι 𝒜 f hf Nat.one_pos).opensRange from hrange.symm]
364	    exact ⟨p, rfl⟩
365	  -- Step (d): build the section in `Γ(Proj 𝒜, U)`.
366	  let sec : Γ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left, U) :=
367	    (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f hf Nat.one_pos).hom.hom x01
368	  -- Step (e): embed in the function field via `germToFunctionField`.
369	  refine ⟨((ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.germToFunctionField U).hom sec, ?_⟩
370	  -- Step (f): non-zero via injectivity.
371	  intro ht
372	  -- `germToFunctionField` is injective on integral schemes.
373	  have hinj_germ :=
374	    Scheme.germToFunctionField_injective (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left U
375	  have hsec_zero : sec = 0 := by
376	    apply hinj_germ
377	    rw [map_zero]

406	
407	/-- **The pole divisor of a morphism `φ : C → ℙ¹` of smooth proper curves
408	over `k̄`.** As a Weil divisor on `C`, it is `φ^*[∞]` — the pullback of the
409	divisor `[∞]` on `ℙ¹` along `φ`.
410	
411	**iter-190 substantive body (Pin 2 corrective Option (a) — positive part of
412	principal).** The body is the *positive part* of the principal Weil divisor
413	on `C` of the pulled-back local parameter
414	`algebraMap K(ℙ¹) K(C) (localParameterAtInfty kbar).val`:
415	
416	  `Hom.poleDivisor φ := positivePart (principal (algebraMap K(ℙ¹) K(C) t_∞) _)`
417	
418	Mathematically, `div(φ^* t_∞) = (φ^* t_∞)_0 − (φ^* t_∞)_∞` on `C`, where
419	`(φ^* t_∞)_0 = φ^*[∞]` (the zeros of `φ^* t_∞` are exactly the preimages
420	of `∞`, where `t_∞` vanishes on `ℙ¹`) and `(φ^* t_∞)_∞ = φ^*[0]` (the
421	poles are the preimages of `0`). Extracting the positive part of
422	`div(φ^* t_∞)` exactly recovers `φ^*[∞]` on the nose.
423	
424	**Why the positive-part wrap is essential (Pin 2 structural-conflict
425	diagnosis, iter-189 → iter-190 corrective).** The naked principal divisor
426	`principal (algebraMap _ _ t_∞) halg` has degree zero on a complete
427	nonsingular curve (Hartshorne II.6.10 / Stacks 0BE3,
428	`Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal_degree_zero` of `WeilDivisor.lean`),
429	whereas the RHS `Module.finrank K(ℙ¹) K(C)` of
430	`Hom.poleDivisor_degree_eq_finrank` is positive (= deg φ ≥ 1) at every
431	call site that uses the φ-induced algebra structure. The positive-part
432	wrap restores provability: `degree (positivePart (principal (φ^# t_∞)))
433	= degree (φ^*[∞]) = deg(φ) = [K(C):K(ℙ¹)]` (Hartshorne~II.6.9).
434	
435	The φ-dependence is carried by the `[Algebra K(ℙ¹) K(C)]` instance
436	binder, which at every call site is the canonical `φ`-induced
437	function-field map (per `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` Decision 2).
438	Changing the algebra instance changes the divisor; in particular, the
439	unfolding
440	`Hom.poleDivisor φ ⇝ positivePart (principal (algebraMap _ _ t) _)`
441	is visible to definitional unfolding, *not* opaque behind
442	`Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / empty `proof_wanted` placeholders.

444	**Helper budget**: 1 named typed sorry (`localParameterAtInfty`, the

452	`WeilDivisor.lean` once the parallel iter-190 WeilDivisor prover lands

573	/-- **Degree of a non-constant morphism to `ℙ¹` via its pole divisor**
574	(Hartshorne IV §2 p. 299 opening + II §6.9 multiplicativity of degree
575	under finite pullback).
576	
577	For a non-constant morphism `φ : C ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` from a smooth
578	proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over an algebraically closed
579	field `k̄`, there exists a positive integer `d` (= `deg(φ)`) together
580	with a Weil divisor `D` on `C` (= the pole divisor `φ^*[∞]` of the
581	pulled-back affine coordinate) of total degree `d`.
582	
583	The non-constancy hypothesis is encoded as `∀ Q, φ ≠ toUnit C ≫ Q`
584	(no constant morphism `C ⟶ ℙ¹` agrees with `φ`); this is equivalent to
585	`φ` being surjective onto `ℙ¹_{k̄}` since the image is a closed
586	irreducible subset of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` of dimension ≥ 1, and the only such is
587	`ℙ¹_{k̄}` itself.
588	
589	iter-178+ body: a non-constant morphism between smooth proper curves
590	over `k̄` is automatically finite (proper + quasi-finite). The
591	function-field extension `k̄(ℙ¹) ↪ K(C)` is finite of some degree
592	`d = [K(C) : k̄(ℙ¹)] ≥ 1`. Take `D := φ^*[∞] ∈ Div(C)` via the standard
593	"pullback of a Weil divisor along a finite morphism" operation; by
594	Hartshorne II.6.9, `deg(D) = deg(φ) · deg([∞]) = d · 1 = d`. The
595	positivity `d ≥ 1` follows from non-constancy (else `deg(φ) = 0` would
596	force `φ` constant). Both the finite-pullback operation and the
597	multiplicativity-of-degree identity are project-bespoke sub-lemmas
598	threaded inside the iter-178+ body (cf. the closely related
599	`Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal_degree_zero` of `RR.1`).
600	
601	Blueprint reference: `lem:degree_via_pole_divisor` (Hartshorne IV §2
602	opening, p. 299; II §6.9 p. 137 for multiplicativity).

607	the existential is discharged by ANY positive-degree divisor on `C` and
608	does not reference `φ`. iter-182 strengthens the output to pin `D` to
609	`Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor φ` (a *specific* divisor depending on `φ`) and
610	pin its degree to `Module.finrank (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField

616	`Ideal.sum_ramification_inertia`. -/

622	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
623	    [IsIntegral (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left]

2	\chapter{The rational-curve isomorphism \(C \cong \mathbb P^1\) (RR.4)}
3	\label{chap:RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso}
4	\uses{def:genus, lem:morphism_to_p1_from_global_sections, lem:degree_via_pole_divisor, lem:degree_one_morphism_iso, cor:nonconstant_function_genus_zero, thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero, def:codim1_cycles, def:divisor_degree}
5	% (read from references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 314)
6	% + IV \S2, opening of "Hurwitz's Theorem" (degree of a finite morphism of
7	% curves is the function-field extension degree; read from PDF page 316)
8	% + I \S6 Corollary~6.12 (function-field \(\Leftrightarrow\) smooth-projective-curve
9	% equivalence of categories; read from PDF page 62). The verbatim quotes
10	% pasted in the declaration blocks below were copied character-by-character
11	% from the rendered page images of the scanned PDF (no text layer is present).
12	\section{Setup and motivation}
13	This chapter is the fourth and final sub-build chapter
14	\texttt{RR.1}--\texttt{RR.4} closing the project's headline \emph{RR bridge}
15	\[
16	  \mathtt{genusZero\_curve\_iso\_P1}
17	  \,\colon\,
18	  \text{a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve \(C / \bar k\) with
19	  \(g(C) = 0\) is isomorphic to \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\).}
20	\]
21	The chain assembled across the four sub-build chapters is:
22	\begin{itemize}
23	  \item \texttt{RR.1} (\texttt{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}): the Weil-divisor
24	    group \(\Div(C)\), the degree map
25	    \(\deg \colon \Div(C) \to \mathbb Z\) (\texttt{def:divisor_degree}), and the
26	    degree-zero principal-divisor identity
27	    (\texttt{thm:principal_deg_zero}).
28	  \item \texttt{RR.2} (\texttt{chap:RiemannRoch_RRFormula}): the Riemann--Roch
29	    formula in genus zero \(\ell(D) = \deg(D) + 1\) for \(\deg(D) \geq 0\)
30	    (\texttt{thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero}).
31	  \item \texttt{RR.3} (sibling chapter, dispatched parallel iter-174):
32	    the line bundle \(\mathcal O_C([P])\), its global-sections identification
33	    \(\dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P])) = 2\), and the corollary
34	    \texttt{cor:nonconstant\_function\_genus\_zero} producing a non-constant
35	    rational function $f \in H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P])) \setminus
36	    \bar k \cdot 1\( on a genus-\)0\( curve with a marked \)\bar k$-point.
37	  \item \texttt{RR.4} (\emph{this chapter}): the global classification ``a
38	    smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve \(C / \bar k\) with \(g(C) = 0\)
39	    and a \(\bar k\)-rational point is isomorphic to \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\).''
40	\end{itemize}
41	This chapter consumes \texttt{RR.3} (the non-constant function) and produces
42	the Lean target \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero\_curve\_iso\_P1} pinned at
43	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean} line~290 and restated as
44	the interface block \texttt{chap:RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso} of
45	\texttt{AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex}. The \texttt{RR.4}-closure of that target
46	also drops the \texttt{sorryAx} propagation tagged on
47	\texttt{rigidity\_genus0\_curve\_to\_grpScheme} via
48	\texttt{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}.
49	\paragraph{Scope of the \(\bar k\)-base classification.} The chapter restricts
50	to the case where the base field \(\bar k\) is algebraically closed. Over $\bar
51	k\( every closed point is \)\bar k\(-rational, so the ``\)C\( has a \)\bar
52	k$-rational point'' hypothesis of Hartshorne's Exercise~IV.1.3 is automatic
53	(the smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve \(C / \bar k\) is non-empty,
54	hence has closed points, all of which are \(\bar k\)-rational). The general
55	``\(k \to \bar k\) descent'' step ``genus-\(0\) curve over a non-closed \(k\) with
56	a \(k\)-rational point \(\cong \mathbb P^1_k\)'' is a separate sub-build hosted
57	in \texttt{Jacobian.tex} (the \texttt{genusZeroWitness.key} downstream
58	consumer) and is out of scope here.
59	\paragraph{Standing hypotheses.} Throughout this chapter, \(\bar k\) is an
60	algebraically closed field and \(C\) is a smooth proper geometrically
61	irreducible curve over \(\bar k\). The Lean signature
62	(\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean}) threads the
63	project's standing curve typeclass package:
64	\begin{quote}
65	  \texttt{\{kbar : Type u\} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]\\
66	   (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]\\
67	   \phantom{}\quad[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]\\
68	   \phantom{}\quad[GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]}
69	\end{quote}
70	matching the typeclass set used by the headline target
71	\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero\_curve\_iso\_P1} in
72	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean:290--296}. The genus
73	\(g(C)\) is the one defined in \texttt{def:genus} of \texttt{Genus.tex},
74	namely \(g(C) = \dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, \mathcal O_C)\) via the project's
75	\(\Module \bar k\)-flavoured sheaf cohomology
76	(\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genus}); the concrete projective line target
77	\(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) is the project's
78	\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveLineBar~kbar} of
79	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects.lean}.
80	\section{The morphism to \(\mathbb P^1\) from two global sections}
81	\begin{lemma}
82	
83	\leanok
84	[Morphism to \(\mathbb P^1\) from a globally generating pair of sections]
85	  \label{lem:morphism_to_p1_from_global_sections}
86	  
87	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphismToP1OfGlobalSections}
88	  Let \(X\) be a scheme over a field \(\bar k\), let \(\mathcal L\) be an invertible
89	  sheaf on \(X\), and let \(s_0, s_1 \in H^0(X, \mathcal L)\) be two global
90	  sections whose common zero locus is empty:
91	  \[
92	    \{x \in X : s_0(x) = 0 \text{ and } s_1(x) = 0\} \;=\; \emptyset.
93	  \]
94	  (Equivalently, \(s_0\) and \(s_1\) generate \(\mathcal L\) at every point.) Then
95	  there exists a unique morphism of \(\bar k\)-schemes
96	  \[
97	    \varphi_{(s_0, s_1)} \colon X \longrightarrow \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}
98	  \]
99	  whose pullback of \(\mathcal O_{\mathbb P^1}(1)\) is \(\mathcal L\) and whose
100	  pullbacks of the standard coordinate sections $x_0, x_1 \in
101	  H^0(\mathbb P^1, \mathcal O(1))\( are \)s_0\( and \)s_1$ respectively. On the
102	  basic-open chart $D_+(x_0) = \mathbb A^1_{\bar k} \subset
103	  \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\( the morphism \)\varphi_{(s_0, s_1)}\( is the map ``\)x
104	  \mapsto s_1(x) / s_0(x)\(''; on the chart \)D_+(x_1)\( it is the map ``\)x
105	  \mapsto s_0(x) / s_1(x)$''; the two definitions agree on the overlap
106	  \(D_+(x_0) \cap D_+(x_1) = \mathbb G_{m, \bar k}\) by the cocycle identity
107	  \((s_1 / s_0) \cdot (s_0 / s_1) = 1\).
108	\end{lemma}
109	\begin{proof}
110	
111	  This is the standard linear-system construction in the form of
112	  Hartshorne's Theorem~II.7.1 (``Morphisms to \(\mathbb P^n\)''), specialised to
113	  \(n = 1\). Mathlib (snapshot \texttt{b80f227}) ships the corresponding API
114	  under the name
115	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.fromOfGlobalSections} in
116	  \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic}:
117	  \begin{quote}
118	  \small
119	  \texttt{def Proj.fromOfGlobalSections \{\(\sigma\) A\} [CommRing A]
120	    [SetLike \(\sigma\) A] [AddSubgroupClass \(\sigma\) A] (\(\mathcal A\) :
121	    \(\mathbb N\) → \(\sigma\)) [GradedRing \(\mathcal A\)] \{X : Scheme\}\\
122	    \phantom{}\quad(f : A →+* X.presheaf.obj \(\top\))\\
123	    \phantom{}\quad(hf : Ideal.map f (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant \(\mathcal A\)).toIdeal = \(\top\)) :\\
124	    \phantom{}\quad(X \(\longrightarrow\) Proj \(\mathcal A\))}
125	  \end{quote}
126	  Specialising to \(\mathcal A = \bar k[x_0, x_1]\) with the standard $\mathbb
127	  N\(-grading by total degree, the inputs are a graded \)\bar k$-algebra
128	  homomorphism \(f \colon \bar k[x_0, x_1] \to H^0(X, \mathcal O_X)\) and a
129	  proof that the irrelevant ideal \((x_0, x_1) \cdot \bar k[x_0, x_1]\) maps to
130	  the unit ideal of \(H^0(X, \mathcal O_X)\) (i.e.\ no common zeros). The
131	  resulting morphism lands in $\mathrm{Proj}\,\bar k[x_0, x_1] = \mathbb
132	  P^1_{\bar k}\( (Hartshorne~II, Example~7.1.3, the construction of \)\mathbb
133	  P^n\( as a \)\mathrm{Proj}\(). When \)\mathcal L$ is non-trivial the present
134	  lemma's signature is reduced to the Mathlib API by working on the open
135	  cover \(X = D(s_0) \cup D(s_1)\), where on each chart the sections become
136	  honest global functions (\(s_1 / s_0\) on \(D(s_0)\), \(s_0 / s_1\) on \(D(s_1)\),
137	  agreeing on the overlap). The chart-glue is performed by the
138	  project-bespoke wrapper
139	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphismToP1OfGlobalSections}, whose body
140	  in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean} chains
141	  \texttt{Proj.fromOfGlobalSections} with the chart-restriction-and-glue
142	  pattern of \texttt{Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms}.
143	  Uniqueness follows from the standard separation argument for morphisms to
144	  a separated scheme: two morphisms \(\varphi, \psi \colon X \to \mathbb P^1\)
145	  with \(\varphi^* x_i = \psi^* x_i\) for \(i = 0, 1\) agree on the basic-open
146	  charts \(D_+(x_0), D_+(x_1)\) (where they pull back the affine coordinates to
147	  the same rational function), and these charts cover \(\mathbb P^1\), so the
148	  two morphisms agree globally by gluing.
149	\end{proof}
150	\section{Degree of a morphism to \(\mathbb P^1\) via its pole divisor}
151	% NOTE (iter-190 plan-phase Pin 2 corrective; supersedes iter-182 narrative).
152	% `Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor \varphi` is the Weil divisor \(\varphi^*[\infty]\),
153	% defined as `positivePart (principal (\varphi^{\#} t_\infty))` in Lean.
154	% The supporting `Scheme.WeilDivisor.positivePart` def lives in
155	% `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` (\ref{def:WeilDivisor_positivePart}); the
156	% Hartshorne~II.6.9 identity that powers the existential degree statement
157	% lives there as \ref{lem:degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank}.
158	\begin{lemma}
159	
160	\leanok
161	[Degree of a non-constant morphism to \(\mathbb P^1\) via the preimage of \(\infty\)]
162	  \label{lem:degree_via_pole_divisor}
163	  
164	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor}
165	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], IV \S2, opening paragraph of "Hurwitz's Theorem"
166	  % (definition of the degree of a finite morphism of curves) (read from
167	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 316, doc p.~299).
168	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Recall that the \emph{degree} of a finite morphism
169	  % \(f: X \to Y\) of curves is defined to be the degree \([K(X):K(Y)]\) of the
170	  % extension of function fields (II, \S 6)."
171	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, IV.2, p.~299, opening of \S2.}
172	  Let \(\varphi \colon C \to \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) be a non-constant morphism
173	  from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve \(C / \bar k\). Then
174	  \(\varphi\) is finite, its function-field extension
175	  \(\bar k(\mathbb P^1) \hookrightarrow K(C)\) (the canonical
176	  \(\varphi\)-induced map, supplied as an
177	  \texttt{[Algebra (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).functionField C.functionField]}
178	  instance argument on the Lean side) is finite, and there exists a Weil
179	  divisor \(D \in \Div(C)\) such that
180	  \[
181	    D \;=\; \varphi^*[\infty]
182	    \quad\text{and}\quad
183	    \deg(D) \;=\; [\,K(C) : \bar k(\mathbb P^1)\,].
184	  \]
185	  Equivalently, the morphism \(\varphi\) satisfies
186	  \[
187	    [\,K(C) : \bar k(\mathbb P^1)\,] \;=\;
188	    \deg\!\left(\,\sum_{P \in \varphi^{-1}(\infty)}
189	                  \ord_P(\varphi^* t_\infty) \cdot [P]\,\right),
190	  \]
191	  where \(t_\infty \in K(\mathbb P^1)\) is a local parameter at \(\infty\)
192	  (equivalently \(t_\infty = 1/u\) for the standard affine coordinate \(u\) on
193	  \(\mathbb P^1 \setminus \{\infty\}\)); and for any closed point
194	  \(Q \in \mathbb P^1\) the same identification gives
195	  \(\deg(\varphi^*[Q]) = [K(C):\bar k(\mathbb P^1)]\).
196	  \emph{Convention on the pole divisor.} The Weil divisor
197	  \(\varphi^*[\infty]\) is exposed in Lean as
198	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor \(\varphi\)}, a
199	  noncomputable definition introduced (as a typed \texttt{sorry}) at the
200	  iter-182 plan-phase signature strengthening of this lemma. The body of
201	  \texttt{poleDivisor} is queued for iter-183+ closure via the standard
202	  affine-chart construction (\texttt{Ideal.sum\_ramification\_inertia}
203	  in Mathlib, applied at each closed point \(P \in \varphi^{-1}(\infty)\)
204	  to read off the local ramification multiplicity
205	  \(\ord_P(\varphi^* t_\infty)\) from the maximal ideal at \(\infty\) in
206	  \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\)). On the Lean side, the existential output of
207	  \texttt{morphism\_degree\_via\_pole\_divisor} packages
208	  \(D = \texttt{Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor}\;\varphi\) together with
209	  \(\deg(D) = \operatorname{Module.finrank}_{K(\mathbb P^1)} K(C)\),
210	  mirroring the OCofP pattern in which a typed-sorry def
211	  (\texttt{def:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_toFunctionField}) is introduced
212	  upstream of its consumer lemma.
213	\end{lemma}
214	% NOTE (iter-190 plan-phase, supersedes iter-189 review NOTE):
215	% Pin 2 corrective COMMITTED — Option (a). `Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor` is
216	% being refactored to return the *positive part* of the principal divisor
217	% of `\varphi^{\#} t_\infty`, i.e. `(div(\varphi^{\#} t_\infty))_0
218	% = \varphi^*[\infty]`. The supporting Weil-divisor `positivePart`
219	% definition lives in `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` as
220	% `def:WeilDivisor_positivePart`; the substantive Hartshorne~II.6.9
221	% identity that powers `lem:degree_via_pole_divisor` is now pinned as
222	% `lem:degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank` in `WeilDivisor.tex`.
223	% With these substrates in place, `lem:degree_via_pole_divisor` is
224	% mathematically correct as stated: the body's existential witness `D`
225	% is `Hom.poleDivisor \varphi = positivePart (principal (\varphi^{\#}
226	% t_\infty))`, and the degree identity is a one-line corollary of
227	% `lem:degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank`. iter-190 prover lane
228	% (Lane I) implements the refactor:
229	%   1. New def `Scheme.WeilDivisor.positivePart` in `WeilDivisor.lean`
230	%      (~10-15 LOC; lattice `D \sqcup 0`).
231	%   2. New lemma
232	%      `Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank` as a
233	%      named typed-sorry pin in `WeilDivisor.lean` (~5 LOC signature; body
234	%      ~50-80 LOC via affine-chart `Ideal.sum_ramification_inertia` route
235	%      owed iter-191+).
236	%   3. Refactor `Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor` body in `RationalCurveIso.lean`
237	%      from `principal (algebraMap _ _ t.val) halg` to
238	%      `positivePart (principal (algebraMap _ _ t.val) halg)` (~5 LOC).
239	%   4. Close `Hom.poleDivisor_degree_eq_finrank` body via a 1-line
240	%      rewrite consuming (2) (~3 LOC).
241	% Net iter-190 Lane I: 1 axiom-clean closure (Pin 2 body, modulo named
242	% typed sorry on (2)); 1 NEW named typed-sorry pin
243	% (`degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank`) which is itself an
244	% iter-191+ Hartshorne II.6.9 substrate target. See iter-190 sidecar
245	% `iter/iter-190/plan.md` for the full rationale.
246	\begin{proof}
247	  \uses{def:WeilDivisor_positivePart, lem:degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank, def:principal_divisor, def:divisor_degree, thm:principal_deg_zero}
248	  A non-constant morphism between smooth proper curves over \(\bar k\) is
249	  automatically finite. Indeed, the image of \(\varphi\) is a closed
250	  irreducible subset of \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) that is not a point (else
251	  \(\varphi\) would be constant) and not empty, so it is all of $\mathbb
252	  P^1_{\bar k}\( (the only proper irreducible closed subset of \)\mathbb
253	  P^1_{\bar k}\( is a point); hence \)\varphi$ is surjective. A surjective
254	  morphism between smooth proper irreducible curves of equal dimension is
255	  finite (proper + quasi-finite, where quasi-finiteness comes from the
256	  fact that \(\varphi\) has zero-dimensional fibres: a positive-dimensional
257	  fibre would force a one-dimensional irreducible component in the fibre,
258	  contradicting irreducibility of \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) and \(C\) at
259	  generic points). Hence the function-field extension
260	  \(\bar k(\mathbb P^1) = \varphi^\# K(\mathbb P^1) \hookrightarrow K(C)\)
261	  is finite.
262	  The identification \(\deg(\varphi^*[Q]) = [K(C) : \bar k(\mathbb P^1)]\) for
263	  any closed point \(Q \in \mathbb P^1\) is the multiplicativity of the
264	  degree under finite pullback (Hartshorne~II.6.9): for a finite morphism
265	  \(\varphi \colon C \to C'\) of smooth proper curves and any divisor $D \in
266	  \Div(C')\(, \)\deg(\varphi^* D) = \deg(\varphi) \cdot \deg(D)$. Specialising
267	  to \(D = [Q]\) (a divisor of degree \(1\) since \(\kappa(Q) = \bar k\)) gives
268	  \(\deg(\varphi^*[Q]) = \deg(\varphi)\). Equivalently, the degree of
269	  \(\varphi\) equals the number of preimages of any closed point of $\mathbb
270	  P^1\(, counted with the ramification multiplicities \)\ord_P(\varphi^*
271	  t_\infty)\( at the preimage points \)P \in \varphi^{-1}(\infty)$.
272	  \emph{Sub-build note.} The lemma threads two auxiliary inputs:
273	  ``non-constant morphism between smooth proper curves is finite'' (the
274	  surjective-and-proper-and-quasi-finite argument above; Hartshorne~II.6.8)
275	  and ``multiplicativity of degree under finite pullback'' (Hartshorne~II.6.9
276	  and the multiplicativity of \(\deg\) under composition of \(\div\) and
277	  \(\deg\); the closely related ``\(\mathrm{div}(f)\) has degree zero'' specialisation
278	  is \texttt{thm:principal_deg_zero} of \texttt{RR.1}). Both are separable
279	  sub-lemmas inside \texttt{RR.4}'s Lean file
280	  (\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean}) and are
281	  slotted for closure in a follow-up iteration after the
282	  \texttt{morphism\_degree\_via\_pole\_divisor} skeleton lands.
283	\end{proof}
284	\section{A degree-\(1\) morphism between smooth proper curves is an isomorphism}
285	% NOTE (iter-181 Lane I signature refinement; see analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md
286	% Decision 2, verdict DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY). The iter-177 file-skeleton
287	% placeholder hypothesis "Nonempty (C'.functionField ≃+* C.functionField)"
288	% (an abstract function-field iso, unrelated to \(\varphi\)) was strictly
289	% weaker than the birational-extension body needs and was refined in
290	% iter-181 to the explicit function-field-extension-degree formulation
291	% [K(C):K(C')] = 1, i.e. Module.finrank C'.functionField C.functionField
292	% = 1, with C.functionField a C'.functionField-algebra via the canonical
293	% \varphi-induced map (Scheme.Hom.stalkMap at the generic point composed
294	% with IsFractionRing.lift). The proof-prose body below (the Hartshorne
295	% I.6.12 chain + scheme-theoretic alternative argument) is unchanged ---
296	% only the input shape changes.
297	% NOTE (iter-194 reviewer): The Lean `iso_of_degree_one` body inline
298	% Step 1 was refactored iter-194 from 16 LOC of `Subalgebra.bot_eq_top
299	% _of_finrank_eq_one + Algebra.surjective_algebraMap_iff + RingHom.in%
300	% jective + RingEquiv.ofBijective` to 4 LOC consuming two new
301	% axiom-clean substrate helpers (`algebraMap_bijective_of_finrank_one`
302	% + `phi_left_functionField_algEquiv_of_finrank_one`).  Helper (a)
303	% `phi_left_locallyQuasiFinite_of_finrank_one` body was reduced via
304	% `LocallyQuasiFinite.of_fiberToSpecResidueField` to a per-fibre LQF
305	% statement; per-fibre case (\textit{smooth-dim-1} $\Rightarrow$ 0-dim
306	% fibre) remains a Mathlib gap (iter-200 mathlib-analogist sweep
307	% candidate).  Helper (d)
308	% `phi_left_fromNormalization_isIso_of_smoothProper_finrank_one` is
309	% gated on project-side `IsNormalScheme` substrate.
310	\begin{lemma}
311	
312	\leanok
313	[Degree-\(1\) morphism between smooth proper curves is an isomorphism]
314	  \label{lem:degree_one_morphism_iso}
315	  
316	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.iso_of_degree_one}
317	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], I \S6 Corollary~6.12 (function-field-to-curve
318	  % equivalence of categories) (read from
319	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 62, doc p.~45)
320	  % + [Hartshorne], IV \S2 opening paragraph (degree of a finite morphism
321	  % of curves is the function-field extension degree) (read from
322	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 316, doc p.~299).
323	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Corollary 6.12. \emph{The following three categories are
324	  % equivalent:}
325	  % (i) \emph{nonsingular projective curves, and dominant morphisms;}
326	  % (ii) \emph{quasi-projective curves, and dominant rational maps;}
327	  % (iii) \emph{function fields of dimension 1 over \(k\), and \(k\)-homomorphisms.}"
328	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Recall that the \emph{degree} of a finite morphism
329	  % \(f: X \to Y\) of curves is defined to be the degree \([K(X):K(Y)]\) of the
330	  % extension of function fields (II, \S 6)."
331	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, I.6 Corollary~6.12 (p.~45) + IV.2 opening
332	  (p.~299).}
333	  Let \(C, C'\) be smooth proper geometrically irreducible curves over an
334	  algebraically closed field \(\bar k\), and let \(\varphi \colon C \to C'\) be
335	  a non-constant morphism whose induced function-field extension is of
336	  degree one,
337	  \[
338	    [\,K(C) : K(C')\,] \;=\; 1,
339	  \]
340	  formally
341	  \(\operatorname{Module.finrank}_{K(C')} K(C) = 1\) in the Lean signature.
342	  Then \(\varphi\) is an isomorphism of \(\bar k\)-schemes.
343	  \emph{Convention on the function-field map.} At each call-site of this
344	  lemma, the \(K(C')\)-algebra structure on \(K(C)\) used to interpret the
345	  degree \([K(C) : K(C')]\) is the canonical \(\varphi\)-induced
346	  function-field map, i.e.\ the composite
347	  \[
348	    K(C') \;\xrightarrow{\;\text{germ}\;}\;
349	    \mathcal O_{C', \varphi(\xi_C)}
350	    \;\xrightarrow{\;\varphi^\#\;}\;
351	    \mathcal O_{C, \xi_C}
352	    \;\xrightarrow{\;\text{frac.}\,\text{lift}\;}\; K(C)
353	  \]
354	  of the germ at the image of the generic point \(\xi_C \in C\) with the
355	  stalk-map of \(\varphi\) at \(\xi_C\) and the fraction-field lift at
356	  \(\xi_C\) (per \texttt{analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md}, Decision~2). On
357	  the Lean side this canonical instance is carried as a
358	  \texttt{[Algebra C'.functionField C.functionField]} instance argument
359	  to \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.iso\_of\_degree\_one}; consumers
360	  supply it explicitly at each call-site.
361	\end{lemma}
362	% NOTE (iter-189 review): Pin 3 Step 1 (function-field iso K(C') ≃+* K(C)
363	% from `Module.finrank = 1`) closed axiom-clean inline iter-189 (~10 LOC via
364	% `Subalgebra.bot_eq_top_of_finrank_eq_one` + `Algebra.surjective_algebraMap_iff`
365	% + `RingHom.injective` + `RingEquiv.ofBijective`). Step 2 (scheme-level lift
366	% via `Scheme.Hom.toNormalization`) remains a typed sorry in the body, with 4
367	% sub-obligations (a)-(d) documented inline at the sorry. Iter-190+ closure
368	% budget ~80-150 LOC per `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md`.
369	\begin{proof}
370	
371	  The function-field hypothesis \([K(C) : K(C')] = 1\) states that the
372	  canonical \(\varphi\)-induced map \(\varphi^\# \colon K(C') \to K(C)\) is a
373	  finite extension of degree one; since \(K(C')\) is a field and the
374	  extension has dimension one as a \(K(C')\)-vector space, \(\varphi^\#\) is
375	  an isomorphism of function fields \(K(C') \cong K(C)\). (Equivalently, by
376	  the IV.2-opening identification \(\deg(\varphi) = [K(C):K(C')]\), this is
377	  the statement \(\deg(\varphi) = 1\); the morphism \(\varphi\) is finite in
378	  this case by the standard argument that a non-constant morphism of
379	  smooth proper curves is finite, identical to the
380	  \texttt{lem:degree_via_pole_divisor} preamble.)
381	  By Hartshorne's Corollary~I.6.12, the assignment \(C \mapsto \bar k(C)\) is
382	  an equivalence of categories between smooth projective curves over $\bar
383	  k$ (with dominant morphisms) and function fields of transcendence degree
384	  one over \(\bar k\) (with \(\bar k\)-homomorphisms). Under this equivalence,
385	  the isomorphism \(\bar k(C') \cong \bar k(C)\) produced by the previous
386	  paragraph lifts to a unique isomorphism of smooth proper curves $C \to
387	  C'\(, and this isomorphism agrees with \)\varphi$ on the generic point (by
388	  construction: it is the inverse-image of \(\varphi^\#\) under the
389	  equivalence). The two morphisms \(C \to C'\) that agree on the generic
390	  point of the integral scheme \(C\) agree everywhere (a morphism out of an
391	  integral scheme to a separated scheme is determined by its restriction
392	  to the generic point, Hartshorne~II Ex.~4.2). Hence \(\varphi\) is itself
393	  the lift, and is therefore an isomorphism.
394	  \emph{Alternative direct argument.} Without invoking the
395	  equivalence-of-categories framing, the same conclusion is obtained
396	  scheme-theoretically: a finite degree-\(1\) morphism $\varphi \colon C \to
397	  C'$ between integral normal noetherian schemes is an isomorphism.
398	  Indeed, the direct image \(\varphi_* \mathcal O_C\) is a coherent
399	  \(\mathcal O_{C'}\)-module that is generically of rank $\deg(\varphi) =
400	  1\(, and an inclusion \)\mathcal O_{C'} \hookrightarrow \varphi_*
401	  \mathcal O_C\( of coherent rank-\)1$ sheaves whose generic ranks agree
402	  is an isomorphism over the locus where the quotient
403	  \(\varphi_* \mathcal O_C / \mathcal O_{C'}\) has trivial stalk. The
404	  quotient is a torsion coherent \(\mathcal O_{C'}\)-module of generic rank
405	  \(\deg(\varphi) - 1 = 0\), hence is itself torsion-free (a
406	  finitely-generated torsion module over a Dedekind domain of generic
407	  rank zero is zero), so vanishes. Therefore $\varphi_* \mathcal O_C =
408	  \mathcal O_{C'}\( and \)\varphi$ is the structure morphism of an
409	  isomorphism. Cf.\ Stacks tag~\textbf{0AVX} for the categorical version.
410	  \emph{Sub-build note.} The Lean signature
411	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.iso\_of\_degree\_one} consumes a
412	  non-constant morphism \(\varphi \colon C \to C'\) together with a
413	  \texttt{[Algebra C'.functionField C.functionField]} instance argument
414	  (the canonical \(\varphi\)-induced function-field map) and the
415	  function-field-extension-degree-one hypothesis
416	  \texttt{Module.finrank C'.functionField C.functionField = 1}, and
417	  produces an isomorphism \(C \cong C'\) in the slice category
418	  \(\mathtt{Over (Spec (.of\ kbar))}\). The proof body threads the Mathlib
419	  API \texttt{IsIntegralHom.iso\_of\_isUnit} (or the equivalent
420	  \texttt{IsAffineHom + Finite + RankOne} chain) and, where needed, the
421	  project-bespoke ``finite-morphism-of-degree-one is an isomorphism''
422	  helper. The Hartshorne~I.6.12 equivalence-of-categories framing is the
423	  intended high-level proof structure; the scheme-theoretic argument
424	  above is the spelled-out form for the Lean body.
425	\end{proof}
426	\section{Headline: genus-\(0\) curve over \(\bar k\) is isomorphic to \(\mathbb P^1\)}
427	\begin{theorem}
428	
429	\leanok
430	[Genus-\(0\) curve over \(\bar k\) is isomorphic to \(\mathbb P^1\)]
431	  \label{thm:genus_zero_curve_iso_p1}
432	  
433	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero_curve_iso_P1}
434	  % already know that \(p_a(\mathbf P^1) = 0\) (I, Ex. 7.2), so conversely
435	  % suppose given a curve \(X\) of genus 0. Let \(P, Q\) be two distinct
436	  % points of \(X\) and apply Riemann-Roch to the divisor \(D = P - Q\).
437	  % Since \(\deg(K - D) = -2\), using (1.3.3) above, we have \(l(K - D) = 0\),
438	  % and so we find \(l(D) = 1\). But \(D\) is a divisor of degree 0, so by
439	  % (1.2) we have \(D \sim 0\), in other words \(P \sim Q\). But this implies
440	  % \(X\) is rational (II, 6.10.1)."
441	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, IV.1, Example~1.3.5, p.~297.}
442	  Let \(\bar k\) be an algebraically closed field, and let \(C\) be a smooth
443	  proper geometrically irreducible curve over \(\bar k\) with \(g(C) = 0\).
444	  Then there exists an isomorphism of \(\bar k\)-schemes
445	  \[
446	    \varphi \colon C \stackrel{\sim}{\longrightarrow} \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}.
447	  \]
448	  Concretely, the Lean signature
449	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero\_curve\_iso\_P1} (the existing pinned
450	  target at \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean:290}) is
451	  closed by the proof below; the existing interface block
452	  \texttt{chap:RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso} of
453	  \texttt{chap:avr_for_rr} is its consumer-side restatement,
454	  pinning the same Lean declaration.
455	\end{theorem}
456	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the verbatim Example IV.1.3.5 text in the
457	% statement block above (Hartshorne states the classification and its
458	% proof inside the single Example; the proof is the second half beginning
459	% "We already know that \(p_a(\mathbf P^1) = 0\) \ldots"). The present
460	% proof body restates Hartshorne's argument in the project's notation
461	% (linear-system construction \(C \to \mathbb P^1\) via $H^0(C, \mathcal O_C
462	% ([P]))\(, degree-\)1$ identification, isomorphism).
463	\begin{proof}
464	  Over the algebraically closed field \(\bar k\) the smooth proper
465	  geometrically irreducible curve \(C\) has closed points, all of which are
466	  \(\bar k\)-rational (the residue field of a closed point of a curve over
467	  \(\bar k\) is a finite extension of \(\bar k\), hence is \(\bar k\) itself by
468	  algebraic closedness). Fix one such \(\bar k\)-rational point \(P \in C\);
469	  this is the marked point used to build the linear system below.
470	  \emph{Step 1: non-constant function \(f \in H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P]))\).}
471	  By Hartshorne's Riemann--Roch in genus zero
472	  (\texttt{thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero} of \texttt{RR.2}) applied to the
473	  effective divisor \([P]\) of degree \(1\), we obtain
474	  \(\ell([P]) = \deg([P]) + 1 = 2\). The constant section
475	  \(1 \in \bar k \cdot 1 \subset H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P]))\) accounts for a
476	  one-dimensional \(\bar k\)-subspace; the two-dimensional quotient supplies
477	  a non-constant section $f \in H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P])) \setminus
478	  \bar k \cdot 1$. This is the corollary
479	  \texttt{cor:nonconstant\_function\_genus\_zero} of \texttt{RR.3} (sibling
480	  chapter, \texttt{RiemannRoch\_OCofP.tex}).
481	  \emph{Step 2: morphism \(C \to \mathbb P^1\) via the linear system \((1, f)\).}
482	  The pair \((1, f) \in H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P]))^2\) generates the
483	  invertible sheaf \(\mathcal O_C([P])\) at every point of \(C\): at points
484	  \(Q \in C \setminus \{P\}\) the section \(1\) does not vanish in
485	  \(\mathcal O_C([P])_Q \cong \mathcal O_{C, Q}\), and at the marked point
486	  \(P\) the section \(f\) does not vanish in \(\mathcal O_C([P])_P\) (because \(f\)
487	  has a pole of order exactly \(1\) at \(P\) in the rational-function
488	  picture: \(f \in K(C) \setminus \bar k\) with \(\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty = [P]\)).
489	  Therefore by \texttt{lem:morphism_to_p1_from_global_sections} there
490	  exists a unique morphism
491	  \[
492	    \varphi \;:=\; \varphi_{(1, f)} \colon C \longrightarrow \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}
493	  \]
494	  with \(\varphi^* x_0 = 1\) and \(\varphi^* x_1 = f\) (so the pullback of
495	  the standard ratio coordinate \(u = x_1 / x_0\) on $\mathbb P^1 \setminus
496	  \{\infty\}\( is \)f$, and the pullback of the inverse coordinate
497	  \(t_\infty = x_0 / x_1 = 1 / f\) is the local parameter \(1/f\) at \(P\)).
498	  \emph{Step 3: \(\deg(\varphi) = 1\) via the pole divisor of \(f\).} The
499	  morphism \(\varphi\) is non-constant (since \(f\) is non-constant as a
500	  rational function on \(C\), the pullback \(\varphi^* u = f\) takes
501	  infinitely many values, ruling out a constant \(\varphi\)). By
502	  \texttt{lem:degree_via_pole_divisor} applied to \(\varphi\),
503	  \[
504	    \deg(\varphi)
505	    \;=\;
506	    \deg\!\left(\,\sum_{Q \in \varphi^{-1}(\infty)}
507	                  \ord_Q(\varphi^* t_\infty) \cdot [Q]\,\right).
508	  \]
509	  We compute the pole divisor of \(f\) as a rational function on \(C\):
510	  since \(f \in H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P]))\) with \(f\) non-constant and
511	  \(\mathcal O_C([P])\) the line bundle of the effective divisor \([P]\), the
512	  rational-function \(f\) has pole divisor \((f)_\infty = [P]\) (the
513	  unique pole of order \(1\) at \(P\), no other poles; this is the standard
514	  identification of \(H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(D))\) with the rational functions
515	  \(g \in K(C)\) satisfying \(\mathrm{div}(g) + D \geq 0\), specialised to
516	  \(D = [P]\)). Hence \(\varphi^{-1}(\infty) = \{P\}\) with multiplicity~\(1\)
517	  and
518	  \[
519	    \deg(\varphi) \;=\; \deg([P]) \;=\; 1.
520	  \]
521	  \emph{Step 4: \(\varphi\) is an isomorphism.} By Hartshorne's IV.2
522	  identification \(\deg(\varphi) = [K(C) : \bar k(\mathbb P^1)]\), the
523	  degree-one conclusion of Step~3 rewrites as
524	  \(\operatorname{Module.finrank}_{\bar k(\mathbb P^1)} K(C) = 1\) (with
525	  the canonical \(\varphi\)-induced
526	  \texttt{[Algebra \,(ProjectiveLineBar kbar).functionField\,
527	   C.functionField]} instance). By
528	  \texttt{lem:degree_one_morphism_iso} applied to the non-constant
529	  morphism \(\varphi \colon C \to \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) (smooth proper
530	  geometrically irreducible curves over \(\bar k\) on both sides), under
531	  this function-field-extension-degree-one hypothesis, \(\varphi\) is an
532	  isomorphism of \(\bar k\)-schemes.
533	  This produces the required isomorphism \(C \cong \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\)
534	  closing the headline target
535	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero\_curve\_iso\_P1}.
536	  \emph{Comparison with Hartshorne's original argument.} Hartshorne's
537	  Example~IV.1.3.5 phrases the argument in terms of two distinct points
538	  \(P, Q \in C\) and the degree-zero divisor \(D = P - Q\), deriving \(P \sim Q\)
539	  and concluding via II.6.10.1 that \(C\) is rational; combined with I.6.12
540	  this yields \(C \cong \mathbb P^1\). The present proof restates the same
541	  combinatorial content via the more directly formalisable linear-system
542	  construction: instead of going through the \(P \sim Q\) statement and
543	  invoking II.6.10.1 as a black box, we produce the explicit morphism
544	  \(\varphi \colon C \to \mathbb P^1\) from the two sections \((1, f)\) of
545	  \(\mathcal O_C([P])\) given by Riemann--Roch and identify its degree
546	  directly. The two arguments are mathematically equivalent and have the
547	  same dependency footprint (Riemann--Roch in genus zero \(\Rightarrow\)
548	  \(\ell([P]) = 2\) \(\Rightarrow\) non-constant \(f\) \(\Rightarrow\) degree-\(1\)
549	  map to \(\mathbb P^1\) \(\Rightarrow\) iso); the linear-system phrasing maps
550	  more cleanly onto the Mathlib \texttt{Proj.fromOfGlobalSections} API and
551	  the project's existing Weil-divisor/degree pipeline of \texttt{RR.1}.
552	\end{proof}

554	[Drop of \texttt{sorryAx} on the genus-\(0\) Albanese keystone]
555	  \label{rmk:rr4_unblocks_sorryAx_on_rigidity_genus0}

567	\section{Out of scope}
568	The following items are deliberately \emph{not} part of \texttt{RR.4} and
569	are sequenced elsewhere:
570	\begin{itemize}
571	  \item The general ``smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve \(C\) of
572	    genus \(0\) over a \emph{non-closed} field \(k\) with a \(k\)-rational point
573	    \(\cong \mathbb P^1_k\)'' statement is the \(\bar k \to k\) descent of
574	    \texttt{chap:RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso}. The descent is hosted in
575	    \texttt{Jacobian.tex} via \texttt{genusZeroWitness.key}; it consumes
576	    \texttt{chap:RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso} together with Galois descent for
577	    smooth proper curves and is out of scope for \texttt{RR.4}.
578	  \item The Mathlib API \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.fromOfGlobalSections}
579	    (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic}) is
580	    consumed as a black box by
581	    \texttt{lem:morphism_to_p1_from_global_sections}; its own proof inside
582	    Mathlib (the universal property of \(\mathrm{Proj}\), graded-ring
583	    presentation of \(\mathbb P^n\)) is not re-derived here.
584	  \item Multiplicativity of degree under finite pullback (Hartshorne~II.6.9)
585	    is consumed as a black box by \texttt{lem:degree_via_pole_divisor};
586	    its proof is a separable sub-lemma threaded inside
587	    \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean} and is
588	    \emph{not} part of \texttt{RR.4}'s blueprint statement (it lives at
589	    the same logical level as the closely related
590	    \texttt{thm:principal_deg_zero} of \texttt{RR.1}).
591	  \item The full equivalence of categories ``smooth projective curves over
592	    \(\bar k\) with dominant morphisms'' \(\cong\) ``function fields of
593	    transcendence degree~\(1\) over \(\bar k\) with \(\bar k\)-homomorphisms''
594	    (Hartshorne~I.6.12) is consumed as a high-level framing in
595	    \texttt{lem:degree_one_morphism_iso} but is \emph{not} re-formalised at
596	    the equivalence-of-categories level here. The proof body in the Lean
597	    file threads the spelled-out scheme-theoretic ``finite-degree-one
598	    morphism between integral normal schemes is an isomorphism''
599	    argument (Stacks~\textbf{0AVX}), which is the minimal-Mathlib-dependence
600	    pin compatible with the chapter's downstream needs.
601	  \item ``A smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over \(\bar k\)
602	    has at least two distinct \(\bar k\)-points'' (the non-empty / infinite
603	    cardinality of \(C(\bar k)\)) is consumed implicitly by the present
604	    chapter but its formal statement lives in the project's curve API
605	    (\texttt{Genus.tex} / \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian.Genus}); the chapter
606	    does not restate it.
607	  \item Cohomology of the line bundle \(\mathcal O_C([P])\) on a curve
608	    (Hartshorne~III.5 + IV.1) is fully consumed via \texttt{RR.3}; in
609	    particular the \(H^1\)-vanishing of \(\mathcal O_C([P])\) on a genus-\(0\)
610	    curve is part of \texttt{RR.3}'s payload feeding
611	    \texttt{thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero} of \texttt{RR.2}, and is not
612	    re-derived here.
613	\end{itemize}
